b:T74622,<div class="specification">
<p>Boreal forests stretch across Canada, Russia and Scandinavia. This northern ecosystem accounts for 29 % of the world’s forest areas. The long, cold winters favour tall evergreen trees with either needles or scale-like leaves. These trees are wind-pollinated and their seeds are not enclosed in a fruit. The photograph shows a typical boreal forest in winter.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Identify the dominant plant phylum in the boreal forest.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">In some areas there are gaps in the boreal forest where trees fail to grow and peat tends to accumulate. Suggest reasons for this.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">An increase in global temperatures poses a critical threat to boreal forests. Explain the consequences of climate change to this northern ecosystem.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">The boreal forests are situated close to the north pole and even in summer the intensity of sunlight is lower than at the equator. Sketch a graph showing the effect of light on the rate of photosynthesis, labelling the axes.</p>
<p style="text-align: left;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>c:T77a,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>coniferophyta/conifer/coniferous/gymnosperms/pinophyta ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. waterlogged soil/poor drainage<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>acidic soil<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>anaerobic conditions/soil ✔<br/><br/>b. organic matter is not «fully» decomposed «leading to peat formation»<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>decomposers/saprotrophs less active/fewer in cold «temperatures» ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. higher temperatures so more transpiration/droughts/dehydration/water shortage ✔</p>
<p>b. more forest fires ✔</p>
<p>c. more/new pests/diseases because of the changed conditions ✔</p>
<p>d. competition from trees/plants «that colonize/spread to boreal forests» ✔</p>
<p>e. trees/«named» organisms «of boreal forests» not adapted to warmer conditions<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>trees/«named» organisms migrate/extend range due to the warmer conditions ✔</p>
<p>f. trees die so there is loss of habitat for animals ✔</p>
<p>g. faster decomposition/nutrient cycling «so conditions in the ecosystem change» ✔</p>
<p>h. standing water/floods due to more snow/permafrost melting ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. <em>x</em>-axis labelled as light intensity/amount of light <em><strong>AND</strong></em> <em>y</em>-axis labelled as rate of photosynthesis/rate of oxygen release/rate of carbon dioxide uptake ✔<br/><br/>b. curve starting at/slightly to the right of the <em>x</em>-axis origin and rising rapidly and then more slowly and plateauing but never dropping ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/9/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","9"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"18N.2.SL.TZ0.3"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"18N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","5-3-classification-of-biodiversity, 9-3-growth-in-plants, 4-1-species-communities-and-ecosystems"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">d.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
